message:
refactor(rate_limit): replace f64 token bucket with integer arithmetic

Migrate the token bucket from f64 to u32 tokens with a u64 fractional
accumulator. The previous f64 implementation suffered from precision
loss at high burst values (e.g., burst=1B) where adding
tokens_per_ms * elapsed would lose sub-ULP contributions, causing
tokens to silently fail to refill over long horizons.

The new implementation uses integer-only arithmetic:
- tokens: u32 (exact count, no rounding)
- fractional: u64 (sub-token accumulator, 0..period_ms-1)
- Refill: added_fractional = elapsed_ms * rate; new_tokens =
  total_fractional / period_ms (integer division, no precision loss)
- When tokens == burst, fractional resets to 0 to prevent drift

This eliminates:
- tokens_per_ms precomputation (removed field)
- All f64 comparisons and casts in try_acquire/time_until_available
- Float epsilon checks in tests (now exact integer assertions)

RateLimitMetrics fields available_tokens and current_rate change from
f64 to u32. The middleware test is updated accordingly.

New regression test (tests/rate_limit_float_bug.rs): verifies that
1 token per hour with burst=1B correctly refills after 3600 seconds
of millisecond-granularity polling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
